items.text: How long have you had this fever, how high does it go, and does it come and go in a specific pattern?
choices: Chronic fever lasting more than 3 weeks

items.text: Have you had shaking chills, drenching night sweats, or significant unexplained weight loss?
choices: No night sweats, chills, or weight loss
Chills or night sweats without weight loss
Unintentional weight loss with drenching night sweats

items.text: Have you had a persistent cough, shortness of breath, chest pain, or sinus congestion?
choices: Cough or sinus congestion without dyspnea
Shortness of breath or pleuritic chest pain

items.text: Have you had abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or yellowing of your skin or eyes?
choices: Severe abdominal pain, vomiting, or jaundice

items.text: Have you had burning or pain when you urinate, frequent urination, or flank/back pain?
Have you had new joint pain, joint swelling, muscle aches, or severe headache?
choices: Diffuse muscle aches or mild joint pain
Joint swelling, severe headache, or neck stiffness

items.text: Have you noticed any new rashes, skin lumps, color changes, or sores?
choices: Petechiae, purpura, or nodular skin lesions

items.text: Have you traveled outside the country recently, spent time in wooded areas, or had contact with sick individuals?
choices: No recent travel, outdoor, or sick exposures
International travel to endemic area or tick/insect bite

items.text: Have you had close contact with animals, drank unpasteurized milk, or eaten undercooked meat?
choices: No animal or high-risk dietary exposures
Exposure to farm animals, wild animals, or unpasteurized dairy

items.text: Have you started any new medications or supplements recently, or do you have any implanted medical devices?
choices: No new medications or implanted devices
New medication without known drug fever risk
New drug fever risk medication or implanted device/line

items.text: Do you have a history of cancer, autoimmune disease, or any condition that suppresses your immune system?
choices: No history of immunosuppression or malignancy
Active hematologic malignancy, chemotherapy, or severe immunosuppression

items.text: Assess body temperature, heart rate, blood pressure, and overall clinical appearance.
Inspect skin, mucosal surfaces, and conjunctivae for rashes, petechiae, splinter hemorrhages, or jaundice.
choices: Skin intact, no rash or lesions
Petechiae, purpura, splinter hemorrhages, or jaundice

items.text: Palpate cervical, supraclavicular, axillary, and inguinal lymph nodes.
Auscultate the heart for murmurs, rubs, or gallops.
choices: New diastolic or changing murmur, or pericardial rub

items.text: Auscultate lungs for crackles, wheezing, or decreased breath sounds.
choices: Diffuse crackles, rub, or absent breath sounds

items.text: Palpate the abdomen for tenderness, masses, or organomegaly (hepatosplenomegaly).
choices: Focal tenderness, guarding, palpable mass, or hepatosplenomegaly

items.text: Percuss the costovertebral angles for tenderness.
Examine joints for swelling, erythema, warmth, or tenderness, and inspect temporal arteries.
choices: No joint swelling or temporal artery tenderness
Active synovitis, joint effusion, or tender/thickened temporal arteries

items.text: Assess neck mobility for rigidity or signs of meningismus.
choices: Supple neck, full range of motion
Nuchal rigidity, positive Brudzinski or Kernig signs

items.text: Inspect the dentition, pharynx, and sinuses.
choices: Dental abscess, dental caries, or tonsillar exudates
